tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: After Years of Silence, ALS Patient Speaks Fluently Again Through Brain Implant
stories.summary: Casey Harrell, silenced by ALS two years ago, now speaks fluently for twelve hours at a stretch thanks to a brain implant recording 256 electrodes in his speech cortex.

stories.headline: Nine-Year-Old Oliver Scores His First Try as Opposition Beautifully Fakes Their Tackles
stories.summary: Nine-year-old Oliver scores his first rugby try from his wheelchair as the opposing team beautifully fakes their tackles to honor the moment.

stories.headline: Sanitation Workers Find Starving Puppy in Fallen Garbage Bin, Adopt Him as PJ
stories.summary: Milwaukee sanitation workers Naz Nalls and Omar discover a starving 10-pound puppy in a fallen garbage bin on April 15, rescue him, and adopt him as PJ.

stories.headline: Japanese Golden Eagle Born in Zoo Takes Flight Into the Wild
stories.summary: A young eagle bred in Tokyo zoo flies free for the first time, marking Japan's first zoo-to-wild eagle reintroduction and hope for the species.

stories.headline: Surgery-Recovering Senior Pitcher Returns for Championship, Ends 73-Year Drought
stories.summary: Paxton Schuh returns to pitch just three weeks after meniscus surgery with only a 2% chance of recovery, helping Kaukauna win their first state title since 1953.

stories.headline: Young Supermarket Worker Notices Elderly Customer Struggling, Arranges Safe Ride Home
stories.summary: Phil Snow-Griffiths, 23, notices an elderly customer struggling with heavy shopping at Asda and arranges a taxi to get her home safely with all her groceries.

stories.headline: Arabian Oryx Return to Saudi Reserve After 90 Years, Births Already Recorded
stories.summary: Endangered Arabian oryx successfully breed in King Salman Royal Reserve and thrive, bringing an ancient dream of species restoration to life.

stories.headline: AI Diagnoses 18 Rare Genetic Diseases That Stumped Specialist Teams
stories.summary: OpenAI's o3 reasoning model identifies patterns in previously unsolved cases, giving hope to children with rare illnesses their families thought untreatable.

stories.headline: Argentina's Marsh Deer Rise From Critically Endangered, Wetlands Reborn
stories.summary: Argentina's marsh deer, once critically endangered, now recolonize wetlands across the country, particularly in Esteros del Iberá, as conservation efforts reverse decades of decline.

stories.headline: Single Gene Deletion Makes Hidden Colon Cancer Cells Visible to Immune System
stories.summary: University of Calgary researchers achieve 100% tumor eradication in mice by removing one gene, exposing cancer cells to immunotherapy attack.

stories.headline: Neighbor Honored With $500 Surprise for Consistently Helping Disabled Friend
stories.summary: Jared Hawkins receives recognition and reward for years of quietly helping his disabled neighbor Rita with household tasks and yard work.

stories.headline: Filipino Wildcard Alex Eala Stuns World No. 2 Rybakina at Berlin Open
stories.summary: Rising star Alex Eala defeats a former Wimbledon champion in a thrilling upset, reaching the quarterfinals on sheer determination and skill.

stories.headline: Gharial Crocodile Recovery Soars: 160 Wild Hatchlings and a Released Parent With 19 Young
stories.summary: Nandankanan Zoo celebrates its breakthrough in gharial reintroduction, with wild-born babies and released adults thriving in nature.

stories.headline: After a Decade of Waiting, Alexander Zverev Finally Wins His First Grand Slam
stories.summary: Alexander Zverev, 29, finally wins his first Grand Slam at Roland Garros in his fourth Major final, beating Flavio Cobolli 6-1, 4-6, 6-4, 6-7(5), 6-1.

stories.headline: PE Teacher Guides Young Cyclists Safely Across Dangerous Highway in Weekly 'Bike Bus'
stories.summary: PE teacher Tamara Houser leads a weekly 'bike bus' of young cyclists safely across a dangerous highway intersection to help students get to school.

stories.headline: World's First 100% Hydrogen Ship Engine Certified, Producing Only Water Vapor
stories.summary: Lloyd's Register approves humanity's first marine engine running entirely on hydrogen fuel, eliminating fossil fuels from global shipping.
